fields.description:
Copy the description from YARN-3942: Timeline store to read events from HDFS
This adds a new timeline store plugin that is intended as a stop-gap measure to mitigate some of the issues we've seen with ATS v1 while waiting for ATS v2. The intent of this plugin is to provide a workable solution for running the Tez UI against the timeline server on a large-scale clusters running many thousands of jobs per day.

comments.body:
Hi Xuan,
could you add a bit of detail to this issue? I'm trying to understand what's going on but don't really know what it's about. Thank you!

Most of the details can be found in the discussion in YARN-3942.  Executive summary is that the original ATS has scaling issues, and we need an interim solution until the [ATSv2 effort|https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/YARN-2928] is ready.  The basic idea is to have clients and jobs write entities to HDFS rather than posting them to a single, central web service to better handle the write load from a large cluster running lots of jobs concurrently.  We need something like this to get a working Tez UI on our large clusters and can't wait until ATSv2 is ready.
HADOOP-12906 shows that AuthenticatedURL isn't helping things to work

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

comments.body:
Hi Xuan,
could you add a bit of detail to this issue? I'm trying to understand what's going on but don't really know what it's about. Thank you!

Most of the details can be found in the discussion in YARN-3942.  Executive summary is that the original ATS has scaling issues, and we need an interim solution until the [ATSv2 effort|https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/YARN-2928] is ready.  The basic idea is to have clients and jobs write entities to HDFS rather than posting them to a single, central web service to better handle the write load from a large cluster running lots of jobs concurrently.  We need something like this to get a working Tez UI on our large clusters and can't wait until ATSv2 is ready.
HADOOP-12906 shows that AuthenticatedURL isn't helping things to work
